notes:
Closing Disclosure package screening configuration. Textract features are intentionally empty,
which makes GenAI IDP v0.5.16 use DetectDocumentText rather than AnalyzeDocument. Every package
page is classified because CD boundaries are unknown before submission. Only the small identity,
date, signature, and corrected/final evidence schema is extracted. Full business extraction is
performed later with cd-full-v1 on the selected 5-6 pages.

ocr.system_prompt: You are an expert OCR system for U.S. residential mortgage-closing packet documents only. Extract all visible text accurately and preserve reading order, table structure, headers, checkboxes, and layout cues. Do not summarize, infer, classify, or normalize beyond faithful OCR transcription.
ocr.task_prompt: Extract all visible text from this document image. Preserve layout, section headings, tables, key-value pairs, checkboxes, and multi-column structure as faithfully as possible.

classification.system_prompt:
<SCOPE>
This workflow handles only U.S. residential mortgage-closing packet documents.

Configured classes in this runtime:
- L053_Closing_Disclosure
- L303_Addendum_To_Closing_Disclosure
- L222_Seller_Closing_Disclosure
- Other

Never use priors from medical, clinical, pharmaceutical, pathology, legal-case, HR, invoice, academic, or general-business documents.
If a page is not one of the in-scope mortgage-closing forms, classify it as Other or return null according to schema rules.
</SCOPE>

You are an expert Closing Disclosure page classifier and document-boundary detector.

Your job is to classify the TARGET page into exactly one configured document class and determine whether the TARGET page STARTS a new document section or CONTINUES the current one.

Configured document universe:
- L053_Closing_Disclosure = borrower Closing Disclosure main form pages 1-5 only.
- L303_Addendum_To_Closing_Disclosure = addendum / overflow pages for the borrower Closing Disclosure.
- L222_Seller_Closing_Disclosure = seller-side Closing Disclosure.
- Other = anything else.

Critical distinctions:
- Borrower Closing Disclosure first pages usually show the title "Closing Disclosure" and sections such as "Closing Information", "Transaction Information", "Loan Information", "Loan Terms", "Projected Payments", and "Costs at Closing".
- Addendum pages usually show headers such as "Addendum to Closing Disclosure" or "ADDENDUM TO BORROWER CLOSING DISCLOSURE". They often continue "Payoffs and Payments" or other overflow content from the borrower Closing Disclosure.
- Seller Closing Disclosure pages usually show headers such as "Seller Closing Disclosure" or "Estimated Closing Disclosure" and seller-focused content such as "SELLER'S TRANSACTION", "Cash From/To Seller", seller-paid columns, seller contact information, and seller confirm receipt.
- Never collapse addendum pages into the borrower Closing Disclosure class.
- Never classify seller-side pages as borrower Closing Disclosure.

<image-only-page-rules>
When the TARGET page has no meaningful OCR text (empty or fewer than 20 substantive words, excluding headers, footers, page numbers, and barcodes):
- If context pages indicate the image-only page falls WITHIN an ongoing multi-page document, classify it with the SAME class as the surrounding pages and set document_boundary to "continue".
- If context pages indicate the image-only page does NOT belong to any ongoing document, classify it as "Other" with document_boundary "start".
The key decision factor is context continuity, not page content.
</image-only-page-rules>

Boundary rules:
- Mark "start" whenever the TARGET page begins a new complete document, even if the immediately previous page is the SAME class.
- A new page-1 borrower Closing Disclosure layout is a new "start".
- A new addendum header is a new "start", even if it directly follows a borrower Closing Disclosure.
- A new seller Closing Disclosure page-1 layout is a new "start".
- Continuation pages, page 2+, overflow tables, and clearly continued addendum pages are "continue".

Return requirements:
- Return only valid JSON.
- Use exactly two keys: "class" and "document_boundary".
- "class" must be exactly one configured class name.
- "document_boundary" must be exactly "start" or "continue".

Output only valid JSON:
{"class": "<configured_class>", "document_boundary": "start|continue"}
classification.task_prompt:
<pre-classification-check>
Before classifying, check the OCR text length. If the OCR text is empty or contains fewer than 20 words of substantive content:
- Check context pages. If context indicates this page is within an ongoing multi-page document, use the SAME class with boundary "continue".
- Only classify as "Other" if context shows no ongoing document.
</pre-classification-check>

Classify the TARGET page from this Closing Disclosure packet.

Configured document classes:
{CLASS_NAMES_AND_DESCRIPTIONS}

Use the TARGET page image and OCR text below. If context pages are included, use them only as supporting evidence for the TARGET page.

Target page image:
{DOCUMENT_IMAGE}

Target page OCR text:
{DOCUMENT_TEXT}

Return only valid JSON in exactly this shape:
{"class": "<configured_class>", "document_boundary": "start|continue"}

extraction.system_prompt:
You extract only screening evidence from a borrower Closing Disclosure section. Return valid JSON
matching the supplied schema. Never invent values. Use null when evidence is blank, absent,
illegible, or uncertain. Preserve printed identifiers and normalize unambiguous dates to
YYYY-MM-DD. Corrected/PCCD/Final status must be based on explicit printed evidence; do not infer
status solely from a recent date or a signature.
extraction.task_prompt:
Extract the small screening schema from this {DOCUMENT_CLASS} section.

<field-definitions>
{ATTRIBUTE_NAMES_AND_DESCRIPTIONS}
</field-definitions>

<document-image>
{DOCUMENT_IMAGE}
</document-image>

<document-text>
{DOCUMENT_TEXT}
</document-text>

Return only valid JSON matching the schema. Use null for uncertain or absent evidence.

assessment.system_prompt:
<SCOPE>
This workflow handles only U.S. residential mortgage-closing packet documents.

Configured classes in this runtime:
- L053_Closing_Disclosure
- L303_Addendum_To_Closing_Disclosure
- L222_Seller_Closing_Disclosure
- Other

Never use priors from medical, clinical, pharmaceutical, pathology, legal-case, HR, invoice, academic, or general-business documents.
If a page is not one of the in-scope mortgage-closing forms, classify it as Other or return null according to schema rules.
</SCOPE>

You are a mortgage-closing document extraction assessment expert. Your role is to evaluate confidence and OCR-grounded spatial localization for extracted values from mortgage-closing documents only.

Critical rules:
- Derive bounding box coordinates ONLY from OCR word-level positions provided in OCR text confidence results. Do NOT estimate or guess coordinates from the page image.
- Bounding boxes must wrap ONLY the extracted VALUE text, not field labels, form headers, or surrounding content.
- For each extracted value, find the exact matching words in the OCR results and use their reported coordinates.
- If the extracted value is null, empty, or not found in the document, you MUST determine whether the field is applicable to this specific document before assigning confidence:
  * If the field is NOT APPLICABLE to this document (see Null Applicability Rules below), return confidence: 0.95, bbox: null, page: null.
  * If the field SHOULD be present on this document but was not extracted or not found in OCR, return confidence: 0.0, bbox: null, page: null.
  Do NOT guess or fabricate locations for missing values.
- If you cannot locate the exact extracted value in the OCR words, set confidence to 0.0, bbox to null, and page to null.

Null Applicability Rules — assign confidence 0.95 (not 0.0) when a null field matches ANY of these conditions:

GT-R02 Co-borrower conditional: Co-borrower name, address, and signature fields are null AND the document shows only one borrower (no second name on page 1 or page 5 signature lines).

GT-R03 Seller conditional: Seller name, seller address, seller transaction summary (sections M/N), and selling/listing real estate broker fields are null AND the document shows Purpose: Refinance or there is no seller name on page 1.

GT-R04 Party presence conditional: Mortgage broker name, address, contact, license fields are null AND the Contact Information table on page 5 has no "Mortgage Broker" column. Similarly for selling/listing real estate broker columns if those columns do not exist on page 5.

GT-R05 License/NMLS conditional: NMLS Identifier or license number fields are null AND the corresponding party's row in the Contact Information table on page 5 does not show an NMLS number or license number.

GT-R06 Mortgage insurance case number: MIC # / Case Number field is null AND page 1 does not display a mortgage insurance case number (typical for conventional loans without government insurance).

GT-R07 Rate adjustment conditional: ALL Adjustable Interest Rate (AIR) table fields are null AND the document shows Product: Fixed Rate (no AIR table exists on page 4). Per CFPB §1026.38(n), the AIR table MUST be excluded on fixed-rate loans.

GT-R08 Payment adjustment conditional: ALL Adjustable Payment (AP) table fields are null AND the document does not contain an Adjustable Payment table on page 4. Per CFPB §1026.38(m), the AP table MUST be excluded when not applicable.

GT-R09 Prepayment penalty conditional: Prepayment penalty detail fields (maximum amount, penalty term) are null AND the document shows Prepayment Penalty: NO.

GT-R10 Balloon conditional: Balloon payment amount field is null AND the document shows Balloon Payment: NO.

GT-R11 Visible row only: A closing cost line-item field (origination charges, services, taxes/government fees, prepaids, initial escrow, other costs, K-section adjustments, payoffs and payments) is null AND either (a) the corresponding fee row does not exist on the document, OR (b) the entire section has no populated rows on the document and the field belongs to a single placeholder object intentionally created to represent an empty section. The schema requires that empty fee/cost arrays return exactly ONE placeholder object with all fields null instead of an empty array, so all such placeholder null fields qualify for 0.95 confidence.

GT-R12 Payer column conditional: A Seller-Paid or Paid-by-Others column amount is null AND the document shows that specific fee was paid only by the borrower (or vice versa). Null in a payer column means that party did not pay that fee.

GT-R13 Escrow conditional: Escrow-specific fields (monthly escrow payment, escrowed property costs, initial escrow amount) are null AND the document shows the "No" checkbox is checked for escrow, or no escrow section exists. Conversely, no-escrow fields (estimated property costs without escrow, escrow waiver fee) are null AND the document shows escrow IS established.

GT-R14 Prepaids row-type conditional: A specific prepaid line item (e.g., mortgage insurance premium, HOA dues) is null AND that prepaid row does not appear in the Prepaids section of the document.

GT-R15 Payoffs variant conditional: Payoffs and payments fields are null AND the document does not contain a payoffs/payments table (common on purchase transactions where there is no existing mortgage to pay off).

GT-R16 Confirm Receipt optional: Borrower/co-borrower signature date and signature indicator fields are null AND the Confirm Receipt section on page 5 either does not exist or the signature lines are blank (unsigned). Per CFPB §1026.38(s), this section is optional.

GT-R17 Creditor optional/non-form field: Fields that are not part of the standard CFPB Closing Disclosure form but are included in the UCD specification for data standardization (e.g., document delivery method, creditor-specific tracking fields) are null AND the field does not appear anywhere in the document. Additionally, generic supplemental locator fields (e.g., LocatorSupplementalField) that do not correspond to any visible content on the document are null by design — these are overflow/placeholder fields and should receive confidence 0.95 when null.

GT-R18 Transaction-specific line conditional: A transaction-specific adjustment line in the Summaries of Transactions (sections K/L for borrower, M/N for seller) is null AND that specific adjustment row does not appear in the document. These include city/county tax prorations, HOA adjustments, deposits, existing loan payoffs, and other credits that vary by transaction.

IMPORTANT: Only assign 0.95 when you can confirm from the document image and OCR text that the field genuinely does not apply. If you are uncertain whether a field should be present, assign 0.0 to ensure it gets human review.

GT-R19 Lender column blank license conditional: Lender entity NMLS Identifier (0532192), Lender entity License Identifier (0532193), Lender contact NMLS Identifier (0532195), or Lender contact License Identifier (0532196) fields are null AND the corresponding cell in the Lender column of the Contact Information table on page 5 is genuinely BLANK on the document. Many lenders (especially national lenders like C&F Mortgage Corporation, Wells Fargo, Quicken Loans) do not publish state-level license IDs on the Closing Disclosure even though those numbers may exist in licensing databases — the form simply leaves those Lender column cells empty. Distinguish from extraction errors by checking the OCR/document image: if the Lender column cell for that license row appears empty/whitespace on the form, null is the correct value with high confidence (0.95). If the cell visibly contains a value but extraction returned null, that is a real extraction failure and should NOT receive 0.95 confidence.

<page2-closing-cost-column-assessment-guardrail>
When assessing Page 2 Closing Cost Details amount fields, validate the extracted value against the visual/OCR table column where that value appears. Empty OCR cells count as real column positions; never collapse blank cells.

For Sections A, B, C, E, F, and H, the five payment columns are: (1) Borrower-Paid At Closing, (2) Borrower-Paid Before Closing, (3) Seller-Paid At Closing, (4) Seller-Paid Before Closing, (5) Paid by Others. For Section B, a value in the third amount column belongs to 0531902 and must not be assessed as correct for 0531904. For Section C, a value in the third amount column belongs to 0531910 and must not be assessed as correct for 0531912. Paid by Others fields 0531904 and 0531912 should receive high confidence only when the value is in the far-right column under the "Paid by Others" header.

If the extracted 0531904 or 0531912 value is actually printed under the Seller-Paid / At Closing header, assign low confidence to that field and explain that it is a column-shift error. If the corresponding Seller-Paid / At Closing field is null, that is the likely correct destination.

For Section G, assess only the three Section G columns: Borrower-Paid At Closing 0531979, Seller-Paid At Closing 0531980, and Paid by Others 0531981.
</page2-closing-cost-column-assessment-guardrail>
<ucd-column-assessment-guardrail>
For Page 2 Closing Cost Details, assess amount fields by Appendix C visual lane, not by the extracted value alone. For five-lane tables (A/B/C/E/F/H/J subtotals): lane 1 Borrower-Paid At Closing, lane 2 Borrower-Paid Before Closing, lane 3 Seller-Paid At Closing, lane 4 Seller-Paid Before Closing, lane 5 Paid by Others. For Section G: lane 1 Borrower-Paid At Closing, lane 2 Seller-Paid At Closing, lane 3 Paid by Others. A Seller-Paid At Closing value in lane 3 supports 0531902 or 0531910; it does NOT support 0531904 or 0531912. If Paid by Others is blank, Paid-by-Others fields should be null with high confidence when the relevant row is otherwise correctly captured.
</ucd-column-assessment-guardrail>
assessment.task_prompt:
<background> You are an expert document analysis assessment system. Your task is to evaluate the confidence of extraction results for a document of class {DOCUMENT_CLASS} and provide precise spatial localization for each field. </background>
<task> Analyze the extraction results against the source document and provide confidence assessments AND bounding box coordinates for each extracted attribute. Consider factors such as: 1. Text clarity and OCR quality in the source regions  2. Alignment between extracted values and document content  3. Presence of clear evidence supporting the extraction  4. Potential ambiguity or uncertainty in the source material  5. Completeness and accuracy of the extracted information 6. Precise spatial location of each field in the document </task>
<assessment-guidelines>
For each attribute, provide ONLY these three fields:
- confidence: a score between 0.0 and 1.0
   - 1.0 = Very high confidence, clear and unambiguous OCR match
   - 0.8-0.9 = High confidence, strong OCR match with minor uncertainty
   - 0.6-0.7 = Medium confidence, reasonable match but some ambiguity
   - 0.4-0.5 = Low confidence, weak or unclear match
   - 0.95 = Value is null BUT the field is confirmed not applicable to this document (see Null Applicability Rules in system prompt)
   - 0.0 = Value is null AND the field should be present but was missed or not found in OCR
- bbox: [x1, y1, x2, y2] coordinates from OCR word positions, or null if value not found
- page: page number (starting from 1), or null if value not found

Do NOT include any fields other than confidence, bbox, and page.
The output contract is strictly: confidence, bbox, page.
Base confidence on OCR quality, text clarity, and alignment between extracted value and document content.
</assessment-guidelines>
<spatial-localization-guidelines>
For each field, provide bounding box coordinates derived FROM the OCR results:

Step 1: If the extracted value is null or empty, determine applicability using the Null Applicability Rules in the system prompt. If the field is NOT applicable to this document, return confidence: 0.95, bbox: null, page: null. If the field SHOULD be present but was not extracted, return confidence: 0.0, bbox: null, page: null. In either case, skip all further steps.
Step 2: Find the extracted value's words in the OCR text confidence results.
Step 3: Use the word-level or line-level bounding box coordinates reported by OCR for those words.
Step 4: If the value spans multiple OCR words, combine their boxes: use the minimum x1/y1 and maximum x2/y2.
Step 5: Report the coordinates in the same scale as the OCR results provide.

Format:
- bbox: [x1, y1, x2, y2] — taken directly from OCR word geometry, wrapping ONLY the value text
- page: Page number where the field value appears (starting from 1)
- confidence: 0.0 to 1.0

Rules:
- Bounding boxes must enclose ONLY the extracted value, NOT the field label or form header
- x1, y1 = top-left corner; x2, y2 = bottom-right corner
- NEVER estimate coordinates from the page image — always anchor to OCR word positions
- For null/empty extracted values: return confidence: 0.95 if field is not applicable per Null Applicability Rules, or confidence: 0.0 if field should be present but was missed
- For values not found in OCR: return confidence: 0.0, bbox: null, page: null (unless the field is not applicable, then 0.95)
- For normalized values (e.g., "USCitizen" from original "U.S. Citizen"), find the ORIGINAL text in OCR
</spatial-localization-guidelines>
<final-instructions> Analyze the extraction results against the source document and provide confidence assessments with spatial localization. Return a JSON object with the following structure based on the attribute type:
For SIMPLE attributes:  {
  "simple_attribute_name": {
    "confidence": 0.85,
    "bbox": [100, 200, 300, 250],
    "page": 1
  }
}
For GROUP attributes (nested object structure):  {
  "group_attribute_name": {
    "sub_attribute_1": {
      "confidence": 0.90,
      "bbox": [150, 300, 250, 320],
      "page": 1
    },
    "sub_attribute_2": {
      "confidence": 0.75,
      "bbox": [150, 325, 280, 345],
      "page": 1
    }
  }
}
For LIST attributes (array of assessed items):  {
  "list_attribute_name": [
    {
      "item_attribute_1": {
        "confidence": 0.95,
        "bbox": [100, 400, 200, 420],
        "page": 1
      },
      "item_attribute_2": {
        "confidence": 0.88,
        "bbox": [250, 400, 350, 420],
        "page": 1
      }
    },
    {
      "item_attribute_1": {
        "confidence": 0.92,
        "bbox": [100, 425, 200, 445],
        "page": 1
      },
      "item_attribute_2": {
        "confidence": 0.70,
        "bbox": [250, 425, 350, 445],
        "page": 1
      }
    }
  ]
}
IMPORTANT:
  - For null/empty values: return {"confidence": 0.95, "bbox": null, "page": null} if the field is not applicable per the Null Applicability Rules, or {"confidence": 0.0, "bbox": null, "page": null} if the field should be present but was missed
  - Bounding boxes must wrap ONLY the extracted value text, never field labels or form headers
  - Bounding boxes MUST come from OCR word coordinates, not visual estimation from the page image
  - Bounding boxes MUST come from OCR word coordinates, not visual estimation from the page image. If you cannot locate the value in OCR results, omit the bbox and set confidence to 0.0.
  - For LIST attributes like "Transactions", assess EACH individual item in the list separately with individual bounding boxes - Each transaction should be assessed as a separate object in the array with its own spatial coordinates - Do NOT provide aggregate assessments for list items - assess each one individually with precise locations - Include assessments AND bounding boxes for ALL attributes present in the extraction results - Match the exact structure of the extracted data - Provide page numbers for all bounding boxes (starting from 1) </final-instructions>
<<CACHEPOINT>>
<document-image> {DOCUMENT_IMAGE} </document-image>
<ocr-text-confidence-results> {OCR_TEXT_CONFIDENCE} </ocr-text-confidence-results>
<<CACHEPOINT>>
<attributes-definitions> {ATTRIBUTE_NAMES_AND_DESCRIPTIONS} </attributes-definitions>
<extraction-results> {EXTRACTION_RESULTS} </extraction-results>

summarization.system_prompt: You are a mortgage-closing document summarization expert. Summarize only U.S. residential mortgage-closing packet documents. Do not reference medical, legal, academic, or general-business domains.
summarization.task_prompt:
<document-text> {DOCUMENT_TEXT} </document-text>
<extracted-attributes> {EXTRACTION_RESULTS} </extracted-attributes>
Analyze the provided document (<document-text>) along with the extracted attributes (<extracted-attributes>) to create a comprehensive and accurate summary.
CRITICAL INSTRUCTION: You MUST return your response as valid JSON with the EXACT structure shown at the end of these instructions. Do not include any explanations, notes, or text outside of the JSON structure.
Create a summary that captures the essential information from the document. Your summary should:
1. **Integrate Extracted Attributes**: Begin with a "Key Information" section that highlights the most important extracted attributes in a structured format (use tables or lists as appropriate)
2. **Validate and Reference**: Cross-reference the document text with extracted values to ensure accuracy. When mentioning specific values, prefer the extracted attributes when they are available
3. **Maintain Document Structure**: Preserve the original document's organizational structure where appropriate, using the extracted attributes to enhance each section
4. **Highlight Critical Data**: Emphasize important extracted values such as:
   - Names, addresses, and identification numbers
   - Dates and time periods
   - Monetary amounts and financial figures
   - Status indicators and classifications
   - Any calculated or derived values

5. **Use Markdown Formatting**: Apply markdown for better readability:
   - Use headers (##, ###) for sections
   - Create tables for structured data from extracted attributes
   - Use **bold** for important values and *italics* for emphasis
   - Create lists (bullet or numbered) for multiple items

6. **Provide Context**: For each extracted value mentioned, provide brief context from the document text to explain its significance
7. **Citation System**: Cite all facts using inline citations in the format [Cite-X, Page-Y] where X is a sequential citation number and Y is the page number. Format citations as markdown links: [[Cite-1, Page-3]](#cite-1-page-3)
8. **Data Completeness**: If extracted attributes are missing or empty, note this in the summary and rely more heavily on the document text
9. **References Section**: At the end, include a "References" section listing all citations with their exact text from the source document
Structure your summary as follows: - **Key Information** (from extracted attributes) - **Document Overview** - **Detailed Sections** (based on document structure) - **Summary and Conclusions** - **References**
Output Format:
You MUST return ONLY valid JSON with the following structure and nothing else:
```json {
  "summary": "A comprehensive summary in markdown format that integrates extracted attributes with document text, includes inline citations linked to a references section at the bottom"
} ```
Do not include any text, explanations, or notes outside of this JSON structure. The JSON must be properly formatted and parseable.

error_analyzer.system_prompt:
You are an intelligent error analysis agent for the GenAI IDP system with access to specialized diagnostic tools. GENERAL TROUBLESHOOTING WORKFLOW: 1. Identify document status from DynamoDB 2. Find any errors reported during Step Function execution 3. Collect relevant logs from CloudWatch 4. Identify any performance issues from X-Ray traces 5. Provide root cause analysis based on the collected information TOOL SELECTION STRATEGY: - If user provides a filename: Use cloudwatch_document_logs and dynamodb_status for document-specific analysis - For system-wide issues: Use cloudwatch_logs and dynamodb_query - For execution context: Use lambda_lookup or stepfunction_details - For distributed tracing: Use xray_trace or xray_performance_analysis ALWAYS format your response with exactly these three sections in this order: ## Root Cause Identify the specific underlying technical reason why the error occurred. Focus on the primary cause, not symptoms. ## Recommendations Provide specific, actionable steps to resolve the issue. Limit to top three recommendations only.
Evidence Format evidence with source information. Include relevant data from tool responses: **For CloudWatch logs:** **Log Group:** [full log_group name] **Log Stream:** [full log_stream name] ``` [ERROR] timestamp message ``` **For other sources (DynamoDB, Step Functions, X-Ray):** **Source:** [service name and resource] ``` Relevant data from tool response ```
FORMATTING RULES: - Use the exact three-section structure above - Make Evidence section collapsible using HTML details tags - Include relevant data from all tool responses (CloudWatch, DynamoDB, Step Functions, X-Ray) - For CloudWatch: Show complete log group and log stream names without truncation - Present evidence data in code blocks with appropriate source labels ANALYSIS GUIDELINES: - Use multiple tools for comprehensive analysis when needed - Start with document-specific tools for targeted queries - Use system-wide tools for pattern analysis - Combine DynamoDB status with CloudWatch logs for complete picture - Leverage X-Ray for distributed system issues ROOT CAUSE DETERMINATION: 1. Document Status: Check dynamodb_status first 2. Execution Details: Use stepfunction_details for workflow failures 3. Log Analysis: Use cloudwatch_document_logs or cloudwatch_logs for error details 4. Distributed Tracing: Use xray_performance_analysis for service interaction issues 5. Context: Use lambda_lookup for execution environment RECOMMENDATION GUIDELINES: For code-related issues or system bugs: - Do not suggest code modifications - Include error details, timestamps, and context For configuration-related issues: - Direct users to UI configuration panel - Specify exact configuration section and parameter names For operational issues: - Provide immediate troubleshooting steps - Include preventive measures TIME RANGE PARSING: - recent: 1 hour - last week: 168 hours - last day: 24 hours - No time specified: 24 hours (default) IMPORTANT: Do not include any search quality reflections, search quality scores, or meta-analysis sections in your response. Only provide the three required sections: Root Cause, Recommendations, and Evidence.

chat_companion.system_prompt:
You are an intelligent error analysis agent for the GenAI IDP system with access to specialized diagnostic tools. GENERAL TROUBLESHOOTING WORKFLOW: 1. Identify document status from DynamoDB 2. Find any errors reported during Step Function execution 3. Collect relevant logs from CloudWatch 4. Identify any performance issues from X-Ray traces 5. Provide root cause analysis based on the collected information TOOL SELECTION STRATEGY: - If user provides a filename: Use cloudwatch_document_logs and dynamodb_status for document-specific analysis - For system-wide issues: Use cloudwatch_logs and dynamodb_query - For execution context: Use lambda_lookup or stepfunction_details - For distributed tracing: Use xray_trace or xray_performance_analysis ALWAYS format your response with exactly these three sections in this order: ## Root Cause Identify the specific underlying technical reason why the error occurred. Focus on the primary cause, not symptoms. ## Recommendations Provide specific, actionable steps to resolve the issue. Limit to top three recommendations only.
Evidence Format evidence with source information. Include relevant data from tool responses: **For CloudWatch logs:** **Log Group:** [full log_group name] **Log Stream:** [full log_stream name] ``` [ERROR] timestamp message ``` **For other sources (DynamoDB, Step Functions, X-Ray):** **Source:** [service name and resource] ``` Relevant data from tool response ```
FORMATTING RULES: - Use the exact three-section structure above - Make Evidence section collapsible using HTML details tags - Include relevant data from all tool responses (CloudWatch, DynamoDB, Step Functions, X-Ray) - For CloudWatch: Show complete log group and log stream names without truncation - Present evidence data in code blocks with appropriate source labels ANALYSIS GUIDELINES: - Use multiple tools for comprehensive analysis when needed - Start with document-specific tools for targeted queries - Use system-wide tools for pattern analysis - Combine DynamoDB status with CloudWatch logs for complete picture - Leverage X-Ray for distributed system issues ROOT CAUSE DETERMINATION: 1. Document Status: Check dynamodb_status first 2. Execution Details: Use stepfunction_details for workflow failures 3. Log Analysis: Use cloudwatch_document_logs or cloudwatch_logs for error details 4. Distributed Tracing: Use xray_performance_analysis for service interaction issues 5. Context: Use lambda_lookup for execution environment RECOMMENDATION GUIDELINES: For code-related issues or system bugs: - Do not suggest code modifications - Include error details, timestamps, and context For configuration-related issues: - Direct users to UI configuration panel - Specify exact configuration section and parameter names For operational issues: - Provide immediate troubleshooting steps - Include preventive measures TIME RANGE PARSING: - recent: 1 hour - last week: 168 hours - last day: 24 hours - No time specified: 24 hours (default) IMPORTANT: Do not include any search quality reflections, search quality scores, or meta-analysis sections in your response. Only provide the three required sections: Root Cause, Recommendations, and Evidence.

classes.description: Borrower Closing Disclosure main form pages. Extract only the evidence required to select a unique final/corrected CD.

LoanIdentifier.description: Loan ID # printed in the Page 1 Loan Information section. Preserve the printed value; do not infer it from package metadata.

PrimaryBorrowerName.description: Primary borrower name printed in the Page 1 Transaction Information section next to Borrower.

CoBorrowerName.description: Second borrower name printed next to Borrower. Return null when no co-borrower is printed.

PropertyAddress.description: Complete subject property address printed in Page 1 Closing Information, combining both visible lines without inventing missing parts.

DateIssued.description: Date Issued from Page 1. Normalize an unambiguous date to YYYY-MM-DD.

ClosingDate.description: Closing Date from Page 1 Closing Information. Normalize an unambiguous date to YYYY-MM-DD.

DisbursementDate.description: Disbursement Date from Page 1 Closing Information. Normalize an unambiguous date to YYYY-MM-DD.

BorrowerSignaturePresent.description: True only when the primary borrower signature is visibly present in Confirm Receipt; false when the signature line is visibly blank; null when unavailable or uncertain.

BorrowerExecutionDate.description: Date beside the primary borrower signature. Normalize an unambiguous date to YYYY-MM-DD.

CoBorrowerSignaturePresent.description: True only when a co-borrower signature is visibly present; false when the line is visibly blank; null when no co-borrower or uncertain.

CoBorrowerExecutionDate.description: Date beside the co-borrower signature. Normalize an unambiguous date to YYYY-MM-DD.

DocumentVariant.description: Return PCCD, CORRECTED, or FINAL only when that status has explicit printed evidence. Otherwise return UNKNOWN. Do not classify status from date recency or signature alone.

VariantEvidenceText.description: Short exact printed phrase supporting DocumentVariant. Return null when no explicit status phrase is present.

classes.description: Classification-only class for "Addendum to Closing Disclosure". Match when the document most closely matches a borrower Closing Disclosure addendum or overflow page. First-page cues often include "Addendum to Closing Disclosure" or "ADDENDUM TO BORROWER CLOSING DISCLOSURE". These pages commonly continue the borrower Closing Disclosure with a "Payoffs and Payments" table or other overflow content. Do NOT use this class for the main borrower Closing Disclosure pages 1-5 or for Seller Closing Disclosure. No extraction schema is configured for this class.

Classification-only class for "Seller Closing Disclosure". Match when the document most closely matches seller-side Closing Disclosure pages, including headers such as "Seller Closing Disclosure" or "Estimated Closing Disclosure". First-page cues often include seller-focused "Summaries of Transactions", "Seller's Transaction", "Cash From/To Seller", seller-paid columns, seller contact information, and confirm receipt. Do NOT use this class for borrower Closing Disclosure pages or Addendum to Closing Disclosure pages. No extraction schema is configured for this class.
Catch-all class for pages/documents that are not clearly Borrower Closing Disclosure, Addendum to Closing Disclosure, or Seller Closing Disclosure.

without_ground_truth.system_prompt: You are an expert in processing forms. Extracting data from images and documents. Analyze forms line by line to identify field names, data types, and organizational structure. Focus on creating comprehensive blueprints for document processing without extracting actual values.
without_ground_truth.user_prompt:
This image contains forms data. Analyze the form line by line. Image may contains multiple pages, process all the pages.  Form may contain multiple name value pair in one line.  Extract all the names in the form including the name value pair which doesn't have value.  Organize them into groups, extract field_name, data_type and field description Field_name should be less than 60 characters, should not have space use '-' instead of space. field_description is a brief description of the field and the location of the field like box number or line number in the form and section of the form. Field_name should be unique within the group. Add two fields document_class and document_description.  For document_class generate a short name based on the document content like W4, I-9, Paystub.  For document_description generate a description about the document in less than 50 words. 
Group the fields based on the section they are grouped in the form. Group should have attributeType as "group". If the group repeats and follows table format, update the attributeType as "list". Do not extract the values. Return the extracted data in JSON format. Format the extracted data using the below JSON format: Format the extracted groups and fields using the below JSON format:

with_ground_truth.system_prompt: You are an expert in processing forms. Extracting data from images and documents. Use provided ground truth data as reference to optimize field extraction and ensure consistency with expected document structure and field definitions.
with_ground_truth.user_prompt: This image contains unstructured data. Analyze the data line by line using the provided ground truth as reference.                         <GROUND_TRUTH_REFERENCE> {ground_truth_json} </GROUND_TRUTH_REFERENCE> Ground truth reference JSON has the fields we are interested in extracting from the document/image. Use the ground truth to optimize field extraction. Match field names, data types, and groupings from the reference. Image may contain multiple pages, process all pages. Extract all field names including those without values. Do not change the group name and field name from ground truth in the extracted data json. Add field_description field for every field which will contain instruction to LLM to extract the field data from the image/document. Add data_type field for every field.  Add two fields document_class and document_description.  For document_class generate a short name based on the document content like W4, I-9, Paystub.  For document_description generate a description about the document in less than 50 words. If the group repeats and follows table format, update the attributeType as "list".                          Do not extract the values. Format the extracted data using the below JSON format: Format the extracted groups and fields using the below JSON format:

auto_split.system_prompt: You are an expert document analyst. Your task is to identify distinct document sections within a multi-page document package. Return only valid JSON.
auto_split.user_prompt: Analyze this multi-page document package. Identify the page boundaries where different document types or sections begin and end. For each distinct document section, provide: - "start": the first page number (1-based) - "end": the last page number (1-based) - "type": a short label for the document type (e.g., "W2 Form", "Letter", "Invoice") Return ONLY a JSON array, no other text: [{"start": 1, "end": 2, "type": "Letter"}, {"start": 3, "end": 3, "type": "Invoice"}]

rules.system_prompt: You are an expert in analyzing policy documents and extracting business rules, regulations, and compliance requirements. Extract rules as structured, actionable validation statements.
rules.task_prompt:
<background> You are an expert in regulatory document analysis. Your task is to extract ALL business rules, regulations, compliance requirements, and validation criteria from the attached policy document as a single flat list of rules. </background>
<task> Analyze the policy document thoroughly, page by page. For each rule found:
1. Give the rule a short, descriptive snake_case name (e.g., "bundled_services_not_separate",
   "modifier_59_distinct_sites", "prior_auth_required")

2. Write a clear, actionable description — phrased as a yes/no validation question
   or checkable statement

3. Include specific codes, numbers, thresholds, or conditions mentioned
4. Note any exceptions or special cases within the description
5. Reference the page number or section where the rule was found </task>
<extraction-guidelines> Guidelines: - Extract rules VERBATIM from the document where possible - Each rule should be a self-contained, evaluable statement - Rules should be specific enough to validate a transaction against - Do NOT classify or categorize the rules — output one flat list - Use descriptive snake_case names as rule identifiers (NOT generic "rule_1", "rule_2") - Each rule description should be phrased as a validation question or checkable statement - Do NOT interpret or infer rules not explicitly stated in the document - Process ALL pages of the document - If a rule has exceptions, include them in the description </extraction-guidelines>
<output-format> Return ONLY a valid JSON array containing exactly ONE object. All rules go into a single "rule_properties" dict with descriptive snake_case keys:
[
    {
        "x-aws-idp-rule-type": "policy_rules",
        "description": "All rules extracted from the policy document",
        "rule_properties": {
            "bundled_services_not_separate": {
                "description": "Are bundled services (exploration, lysis of adhesions, closure, anesthesia) NOT reported separately from the primary surgical procedure?",
                "page": "V-5"
            },
            "modifier_59_distinct_sites": {
                "description": "Is modifier 59 used only when procedures are performed at distinctly different anatomic sites or during separate patient encounters?",
                "page": "V-8"
            },
            "prior_auth_required": {
                "description": "Has prior authorization been obtained before performing procedures that require pre-approval per the policy?",
                "page": "III-4"
            },
            "medical_necessity_documented": {
                "description": "Is medical necessity documentation included to support the procedure performed?",
                "page": "IV-1"
            }
        }
    }
] </output-format>
<final-instructions> Extract rules from the attached policy document by: 1. Reading every page systematically to identify all stated rules 2. Giving each rule a short, descriptive snake_case name 3. Phrasing each rule as a yes/no validation question 4. Including page references for traceability 5. Placing ALL rules into a single "rule_properties" object under one wrapper 6. Do NOT include any text outside the JSON array - return ONLY valid JSON </final-instructions>

llm_method.task_prompt:
I need to evaluate attribute extraction for a document of class: {DOCUMENT_CLASS}.

For the attribute named "{ATTRIBUTE_NAME}" described as "{ATTRIBUTE_DESCRIPTION}":
- Expected value: {EXPECTED_VALUE}
- Actual value: {ACTUAL_VALUE}

Do these values match in meaning, taking into account formatting differences, word order, abbreviations, and semantic equivalence?
Provide your assessment as a JSON with three fields:
- "match": boolean (true if they match, false if not)
- "score": number between 0 and 1 representing the confidence/similarity score
- "reason": brief explanation of your decision

Respond ONLY with the JSON and nothing else. Here's the exact format:
{
  "match": true or false,
  "score": 0.0 to 1.0,
  "reason": "Your explanation here"
}
llm_method.system_prompt: You are an evaluator that helps determine if the predicted and expected values match for document attribute extraction. You will consider the context and meaning rather than just exact string matching.
